['There are two views on privilege, and you hold the first of them.

1. Everyone has inherent privileges: IQ, good/bad parents, height, explosiveness, country of birth, etc.  These exist, and there's not a lot we can do about them.

2. Everyone starts the same, and privilege is a result of oppression.

The second view is very common.  If you hold it, then privilege is "unfair" in a preventable way.  Not in a "life is unfair" way, but in a "if you weren't oppressive, others would be better off" way', 'I think this is a false dichotomy, and also that it misrepresents what privilege is and what the people OP is referencing believe. I think their views are much closer to:

1. Everyone has inherent differences. But society is set up so as to value some ways of being different, and devalue others. Society unjustly gives more accommodations and support to some ways of being different, while sometimes actively hindering others. _Privilege_ is the extra social value, support, and accommodations enjoy', "I think @icy-automaton has a point. ['I’m sorry, but that is flat out wrong. An amendment _amends_ - i.e. changes - something. An Amendment changes the Constitution.  

And amendment can’t be unconstitutional by definition - and per the constitution itself. 

Edit:  Here is the relevant test from Article V of the Constitution (which discusses the Amendment process):

>... shall be valid to all intents and purposes, as part of this Constitution...

Amendments are to be treated as if they are a part of the Constitution itself. Full', 'When have we ever declared an Amendment unconstitutional?  We never have because we can’t.

["In theory, schools wouldn't opt out of other subjects, but use programming to supplement their current curriculum.  For instance, most schools have some sort of computer class. Interpretations are accountable to facts. Is it a fact that misogyny is a word with a reasonably clear and easily accessible definition? Is it also a fact that most (if not all) of the MRA subs have a high number of regular contributors who frequently promote attitudes and claims that very clearly fit the bill? These may not be easy ques', '"@felt-futon I see where you're coming from but I think we can agree on some facts here. Misogyny has a clear definition and when you see patterns of behavior or言论被截断了。看来你正在讨论关于某种态度或行为是否符合“厌女症”定义的话题。基于提供的上下文，我将继续这个评论：

当看到某些群体中频繁出现的态度和声明明显符合这一定义时，我们不能仅仅将其归结为“主观”。我认为关键在于我们如何解读这些事实。如果事实表明某些群体确实存在明显的厌女倾向，那么我们就不能回避这一点。每个人都有自己的看法，但事实就是事实。"', '""', '"@piercing-team I get what you're saying about the definition of misogyny being clear, but the issue is how we interpret and apply that definition to specific behaviors or patterns within groups. We need to be careful not to dismiss valid concerns just because they're labeled as subjective."', '""', '"@savory-hertz Exactly! ', 'I don’t think the Swiss are saying lobsters definitely feel pain, they are saying they don’t know if they do but they act like they do.
